configure — Configuration tools¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

The Configure class¶

class core.configure.Configure(object)¶

When writing an application, there are many locations which need to be known, where configuration files are to be found, data files, URLs etc... Configure just offers an centralised set of tools to handle these. The idea is to have a set of environement variables built as follows: ROOT_NAME where ROOT is the name of the application and NAME is the variable name, for example: for application verify the variable pointing to the configuration directory is named VERIFY_CONFIG.

path(name,subpath):
Parameters:
  • name (string) – name of the variable to find the path in
  • subpath (string) – subpath to be added to the main path (optional)

This method returns the path pointed to by an enviroment variable with an optional subpath.

Warning :the path returned is a list of one or more locations pointed to by the variable:

Example:

export VERIFY_DB=/data/databases/verify
export VERIFY_CONFIG=/home/jj/config:/usr/local/apps/verify

from semperpy.core.configure import Configure

print Configure.path('db')
  /data/databases/verify
print Configure.path('config','scores')
  ['/home/jj/config/scores', '/usr/local/apps/verify/scores']
file(filename,name,subpath):
Parameters:
  • filename (string) – name of a file to be found in name / subpath
  • name (string) – name of the variable to find the path in
  • subpath (string) – subpath to be added to the main path (optional)

This method call the path method and checks the exstence of the file filename for each location. It return a list of full paths to filename whenever filename exists.

resolvePath(self,path):
Parameters:path (string) – the path to resolve

This method is called internally, but could prove helpful. Three cases can be met:

  • a path starts with “/”, it is therefore an absolute path
  • a path starts with either ”.” or ”..” it is therefore a relative path to the current one, nothing needs resolving
  • a path starts with “~” or “~/” (both with the same meaning) and it is expressed relatively to the home directory of the application define in the variable APP_CONFIG where APP is the name of the application.
variableName(prefix,variable):
Parameters:variable (string) – name of a variable in the context of the current application

This method is also used internally, for example:

export VERIFY_STORAGE=/db/verify

from semperpy.core.configure import Configure
import os

print Configure.variableName('verify','storage')
  VERIFY_STORAGE
print os.getenv(Configure.variableName('storage'))
  /db/verify
